metadata.title: Eastern Mediterranean Warship Deployment - March 2026
metadata.context: NATO and allied warships deployed around Cyprus following Iran-Israel-US conflict escalation beginning 28 February 2026
data_sources: Open source news reporting (Cyprus Mail, Greek Reporter, Army Recognition)
metadata.author: OSINT research compilation

osint_assessment.notes: Stale AIS last in Toulon. OSINT confirms operating near Cyprus since early March 2026.

osint_assessment.reported_by: itamilradar.com, French MoD, Greek City Times
osint_assessment.role: CSG flagship, Rafale M strike/air superiority, E-2C AEW
osint_assessment.notes: Last AIS from 2018 — carrier has been AIS-dark for years. French MoD confirmed deployment to E. Med with full CSG.
Spain announced deployment in early March 2026. No recent AIS captured.

vessels.name: ITS Federico Martinengo
vessels.mmsi_note: MMSI 247364500 belongs to ITS Luigi Rizzo (F595), NOT Martinengo. Cross-referenced via MarineTraffic, ShipSpotting (IMO 4568933 = Rizzo), MarinevesselTraffic.

misattributed_mmsi.source: DataDocked API returned 'NAVE LUIGI RIZZO' for this MMSI

osint_assessment.role: GP frigate, Aster 15/30 + MFRA AESA radar
osint_assessment.notes: Arrived Cypriot waters around Mar 11 with 160+ crew. Previously deployed to Red Sea under EU Op Aspides.

osint_assessment.role: Air-defence screen for Charles de Gaulle CSG, SMART-L + APAR radars
osint_assessment.notes: Only AIS-broadcasting warship with recent position near theatre. Located off Crete, likely screening western approaches. Departed Valletta Mar 9.
Last AIS in Baltic Sea (Nov 2025). Likely confused with sister ship Evertsen (F805) in some infographics. Same De Zeven Provinciën class but different hull.

osint_assessment.role: UNIFIL maritime task force — monitoring weapons smuggling, supporting Lebanese Navy
osint_assessment.notes: Not directly tasked with Cyprus defence but operating in adjacent waters. 7,200t displacement — largest NATO frigate class.

osint_assessment.role: Area air-defence (SAMPSON AESA + Sea Viper / Aster 30)
osint_assessment.notes: Type 45 is the Royal Navy's most capable air-defence platform. SAMPSON radar can track hundreds of targets simultaneously.

osint_assessment.role: CSG flagship — anti-Iran ops, Israel defence, air superiority
osint_assessment.notes: Last AIS off Morocco (Feb 18) heading NE. Almost certainly now in E. Med. Ford CSG includes Aegis destroyers for BMD umbrella.

osint_assessment.role: BMD-capable Aegis destroyer, reportedly intercepted ballistic missile targeting Cyprus
osint_assessment.notes: Located off Crete near Evertsen. Departed Rota Feb 25. One of Ford CSG escorts.

osint_assessment.reported_by: Greek Reporter, Army Recognition, Cyprus Mail, itamilradar.com
osint_assessment.notes: Red Sea Aspides veteran. Crew has frontline experience neutralizing Iranian-design drones using EW jamming instead of expensive interceptors. Rotating out within days, likely replaced by HS Elli (F-450).

osint_assessment.reported_by: Greek Reporter, Naval Analyses, Greek City Times, Cyprus Mail
osint_assessment.role: High-altitude BMD (Sea Fire AESA radar + Aster 30 SAMs)
osint_assessment.notes: Most advanced Greek warship. Brand new — recently commissioned from Naval Group (France). Forms layered defence with Psara: Kimon handles high-alt ballistic threats, Psara handles low-alt drones. Greek PM Mitsotakis visited the ship off Cyprus. Rotating out within days.

key_findings: MMSI 247364500 is misattributed: belongs to ITS Luigi Rizzo (F595), NOT Federico Martinengo (F596). DataDocked API confirmed by returning 'NAVE LUIGI RIZZO'. Martinengo's actual MMSI remains unknown.
HNLMS De Zeven Provinciën (F802) is NOT deployed to Cyprus — last AIS in Baltic (Nov 2025). Infographics likely confuse it with sister ship Evertsen (F805).
Only 2 of 12 tracked warships are actively broadcasting AIS: Evertsen and Oscar Austin, both off Crete rather than Cyprus proper.
All ships reported operating directly around Cyprus are AIS-dark — standard wartime OPSEC.
Greek Navy systematically withholds MMSI from public databases. Kimon's MMSI (237780180) was found via VesselFinder — a rare catch for a brand-new ship.
Hellenic Navy deploying both Kimon and Psara creates a layered defence: Kimon (Aster 30) for high-altitude BMD, Psara (Centaur EW) for low-altitude drone swarms.
USS Gerald R. Ford's last AIS (off Morocco, Feb 18) is over 5 weeks stale — carrier is almost certainly now in E. Med based on OSINT reporting.
French carrier Charles de Gaulle has been AIS-dark since August 2018 — 8 years without public AIS broadcast.
analysis.ais_coverage_gap: 9 of 12 vessels are AIS-dark or severely stale. Real-time maritime domain awareness requires satellite AIS (Spire/Kpler) or SAR imagery (Sentinel-1) to fill gaps.
analysis.upcoming_rotation: Greek frigates Kimon and Psara rotating out within days (as of Mar 27, 2026). HS Elli (F-450, Kortenaer-class) expected to replace. Continuous Greek naval presence confirmed by Defence Minister Dendias.
